scope: Bridge-team and onboard operational language for M3 corpus growth, kept separate from radio-to-shore Sea Speak.

qa_notes.linguist_note: Watch is the duty period, not a clock or casual lookout word.
Handover is the transfer process, not the next watch or report alone.
Helm is steering control, not help or the wheelhouse.
Rudder, helm, propeller, and steering gear remain separate concepts.
Anchor is equipment/action area, not berth or mooring.
Windlass is anchor-handling equipment, not generic winch or anchor itself.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: bow station / stern station / port station / starboard station
qa_notes.linguist_note: Station positions must stay exact.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: bow station / stern station / port station / starboard station
qa_notes.linguist_note: Station positions must stay exact.
Lifebuoy is not lifejacket, fire extinguisher, or fender.
Fire extinguisher must not collapse into other safety equipment.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: hand over watch / take over watch
qa_notes.linguist_note: Hand over and take over watch are opposite workflow actions.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: hand over watch / take over watch
qa_notes.linguist_note: Taking over watch is not handing it over.
Log entry is a record, not port entry or handover itself.
Port helm remains the trained helm order, not ordinary left or station language.
Starboard helm remains the trained helm order, not ordinary right or station language.
Midships is a helm order, not a station or steady-course report.
Steady course is not alter course or midships.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: let go anchor / heave up anchor
qa_notes.linguist_note: Let go anchor and heave up anchor are opposite anchor actions.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: let go anchor / heave up anchor
qa_notes.linguist_note: Heave up/weigh anchor is not letting go the anchor.
Anchor ready is a readiness status, not anchor down or aweigh.
Make fast is securing a line, not letting go or casting off.
Let go lines is not make fast or anchor work.
Stand by command and standing-by status must stay separate.
Standing by is a status, not the command stand by.
Safety check is planned verification, not an emergency action.
Fire, flooding, and man overboard are different emergencies.
Flooding is not fire, deck water, or pump readiness.
Completion, start, takeover, and report completion stay separate.

items.target_text: I take over the bridge watch.
accepted_answers: i take over the bridge watch

should_accept: I take over the bridge watch.
should_reject: i hand over the bridge watch
dangerous_minimal_pairs: hand over watch / take over watch
qa_notes.linguist_note: Bridge-watch takeover must stay separate from handover and engine watch.
Report/request and watch/engine status must stay exact.
Log-entry status must stay distinct.
Port helm order must not become starboard or ordinary left wording.
Starboard helm order must not become port or ordinary right wording.
Midships remains a helm order.

items.target_text: Steady on course zero nine zero.
accepted_answers: steady on course zero nine zero

should_accept: Steady on course zero nine zero.
qa_notes.linguist_note: Steady order, heading digits, and report forms must stay exact.
Order completed is not action completed or receipt only.
Action completed and order completed stay separate for this contrast drill.

accepted_answers: anchor is ready for letting go

should_accept: anchor is ready for letting go
qa_notes.linguist_note: Anchor readiness is not anchor state or line readiness.

accepted_answers: let go the anchor on command

should_accept: let go the anchor on command
dangerous_minimal_pairs: let go anchor / heave up anchor
qa_notes.linguist_note: Anchor action must not become opposite anchor or line action.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: let go anchor / heave up anchor
qa_notes.linguist_note: Anchor aweigh, anchor down, ready, and dragging are different states.
Anchor-down status must stay exact.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: bow station / stern station / port station / starboard station
qa_notes.linguist_note: Station readiness must not become another station or a line status.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: bow station / stern station / port station / starboard station
qa_notes.linguist_note: Station readiness must stay exact.
Make fast/let go and forward/aft lines must stay separate.
Let go/make fast and aft/forward lines must stay separate.
Check target and action must stay item-specific.
Safety check is not use action and equipment must stay exact.
Fire, flooding, and man overboard must stay separate.
Man overboard is an emergency, not person on board or a drill.
Start/stop/check and fire/bilge pump boundaries must stay exact.

examples: I take over the bridge watch.

grammar_patterns.pattern: steady on course + radio course value
examples: Steady on course zero nine zero.

grammar_patterns.pattern: anchor readiness or action on command
make fast or let go + line position

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: hand over watch / take over watch

phrase_onboard_take_over_bridge_watch_00: I take over the bridge watch.
i hand over the bridge watch

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Watch handover and takeover are opposite watch-transfer actions.
Order completion, action completion, and ongoing helm order language must stay separate.
Port and starboard helm orders reverse the vessel steering order.
Anchor handling, mooring lines, and berth concepts must not collapse.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: let go anchor / heave up anchor
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Let go anchor and heave up/weigh anchor are opposite anchor operations.
Securing and releasing lines are opposite mooring actions.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: bow station / stern station / port station / starboard station
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Mooring station positions must stay exact.
Stand by as a command and standing by as a readiness status must stay separate.
Safety checks and emergency actions are different workflows.
Onboard emergency type changes the required response.

producer_notes.status: merged into starter.json after QA acceptance
producer_notes.next_gate: Post-merge validation and QA acceptance before any production deploy
open_questions: Confirm whether onboard helm orders should accept ordinary left/right wording or keep port/starboard helm strict.
Confirm whether handover and take over watch should remain strict opposites in beginner items.
Confirm anchor ready, anchor down, and anchor aweigh status boundaries before regression expansion.
Confirm stand by bow versus standing by should remain a command/status contrast.
Confirm whether onboard emergency phrases should remain internal-action language rather than distress radio calls.
risk_focus: hand over watch / take over watch
let go anchor / heave up anchor

decisions: Keep onboard helm orders strict: ordinary left/right wording remains rejected for port/starboard helm items.
Keep hand over watch and take over watch as strict opposite watch-transfer actions.
Keep anchor ready, anchor down, and anchor aweigh as separate anchor-status concepts.
Keep stand by as a command and standing by as a readiness status; do not collapse them globally.
Keep onboard emergency phrases as internal bridge-team/onboard operations, not distress radio-call replacements.
Keep fire, flooding, and man overboard as separate emergency categories.
